title: European training establishes broad geological perspective
summary: Marc's geological education across Munich and Edinburgh provided a broad foundation in earth sciences, recognized through multiple prizes and awards at Edinburgh.

title: From oceanography to applied mathematics
summary: The MIT-WHOI program established fluid dynamics foundations, while Cambridge's DAMTP marked a decisive pivot toward mathematical modeling—a shift that would define his career.

title: Stanford doctorate establishes core expertise
summary: The Stanford PhD in Petroleum Engineering solidified Marc's expertise in porous media, mathematical modeling, and numerical simulation—the technical foundation for all subsequent research.

title: Tectonophysics expands research scope
summary: The Brown postdoc in tectonophysics bridged fluid dynamics and solid earth geophysics, preparing Marc for the interdisciplinary research program he would build at UT Austin.

title: Building a research program from Assistant Professor to Centennial Chair
summary: Joining UT Austin in 2009, Marc established a leading research group in geological porous media, earning teaching fellowships and advancing to the Dave P. Carlton Centennial Professorship.

title: Natural reservoirs as analogues for carbon storage
summary: The Bravo Dome research demonstrated how natural CO2 reservoirs provide crucial insights into long-term carbon storage security—work featured by the U.S. Department of Energy.

title: Extending porous media theory to icy ocean worlds
summary: Current research on Europa and Enceladus exemplifies Marc's belief that fluid dynamics provides a unifying theme across geosciences—from Earth's subsurface to the outer solar system.
